---
title: "Ad Fatigue Monitor"
subtitle: "Meta Ad Performance Tracker"
pagetitle: "Ad Fatigue Monitor — Meta Ad Tracker by Ben Hall"
date: "2026-02-19"
categories: [SaaS, Next.js, Supabase, Claude Code]
---

<p class="lead">
A SaaS tool that monitors Meta (Facebook) ad creative fatigue in real time, scoring each ad's health and alerting marketers before wasted spend accumulates.
</p>

<div style="display: flex; gap: 0.75rem; flex-wrap: wrap; margin-bottom: 2rem;">
<a class="btn btn-primary" href="https://meta-ad-tracker.vercel.app/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
  <i class="fa-solid fa-arrow-up-right-from-square" style="margin-right: 0.5rem;"></i>
  Try Live Demo
</a>
<a class="btn btn-outline-dark" href="https://github.com/benhall1/meta-ad-tracker" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
  <i class="fa-brands fa-github" style="margin-right: 0.5rem;"></i>
  View Source
</a>
<a class="btn btn-outline-dark" href="../docs/Ad_Fatigue_Monitor_PRD.pdf" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
  <i class="fa-solid fa-file-pdf" style="margin-right: 0.5rem;"></i>
  Product Requirements Doc
</a>
</div>

---

<span class="section-label">The Problem</span>

## Why I Built This

Digital advertisers waste thousands of dollars every month on fatigued ads — creatives that audiences have seen too many times, leading to declining click-through rates, rising costs per click, and plummeting return on ad spend. Most marketers only notice the problem after the damage is done, manually checking dashboards when it's already too late.

There's no simple, automated way for small to mid-size advertisers to get early warning signals that their ads are burning out.

<span class="section-label">The Solution</span>

## How It Works

Ad Fatigue Monitor connects to Meta's ad platform and continuously tracks four key performance signals for every active ad:

- **ROAS Decline** (40% weight) — Are you getting less revenue per dollar spent?
- **CTR Drop** (25% weight) — Are fewer people clicking your ads?
- **CPC Increase** (20% weight) — Is each click costing you more?
- **Frequency Saturation** (15% weight) — Has your audience seen the ad too many times?

Each ad gets a composite fatigue score (0-100) and a health status:

| Status | Score | Meaning |
|--------|-------|---------|
| **Healthy** | 0-30 | Performing well, no action needed |
| **Watch** | 31-60 | Early signs of fatigue, refresh soon |
| **Critical** | 61+ | Replace creative immediately |

---

<span class="section-label">Key Features</span>

## What It Does

**Dashboard** — At-a-glance view of all ads with health badges, sortable by status so critical ads surface first.

**Ad Detail Pages** — 7-day trend charts for every metric (spend, CTR, CPC, ROAS, frequency), plus a breakdown of exactly which signals are driving the fatigue score.

**Automated Scoring** — A daily cron job syncs the latest data from Meta, recalculates fatigue scores, and sends email alerts when ads cross into Watch or Critical status.

**Demo Mode** — One-click login with pre-loaded sample data (9 ads across 3 campaigns) so anyone can explore the full product without connecting a real Meta account.

---

<span class="section-label">Technical Details</span>

## How It's Built

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Frontend** | Next.js 16 (App Router, Server Components), React 19, Tailwind CSS 4 |
| **UI Components** | Shadcn/UI (New York style), Recharts for data visualization |
| **Database** | PostgreSQL on Supabase, managed with Prisma 7 ORM |
| **Auth** | NextAuth 5 (Auth.js) with JWT sessions |
| **API Integration** | Meta Graph API v21.0 for ad data sync |
| **Security** | AES-256-GCM encryption for stored API tokens |
| **Deployment** | Vercel with daily cron job scheduling |
| **Development** | AI-assisted with Claude Code |

---

<span class="section-label">Product Thinking</span>

## Design Decisions

**Weighted scoring over simple thresholds** — A single metric spike doesn't mean an ad is fatigued. The weighted algorithm considers multiple signals together, reducing false positives.

**72-hour grace period** — New ads need time to stabilize. The system skips scoring for ads less than 3 days old to avoid premature alerts.

**Hierarchical data sync** — The sync process mirrors Meta's ad structure (Account > Campaign > Ad Set > Ad) and upserts everything, so it's idempotent and safe to retry.

**Demo-first approach** — Rather than requiring Meta OAuth setup just to evaluate the product, the demo mode lets stakeholders experience the full product immediately.

---

<span class="section-label">Learnings</span>

## What I Learned

Building this project deepened my understanding of how performance marketing works at a data level — not just vanity metrics, but the interplay between frequency, audience fatigue, and diminishing returns. I also gained hands-on experience with:

- Designing scoring algorithms that balance multiple weighted signals
- Working with third-party APIs (Meta Graph API) and handling pagination, rate limits, and token management
- Encrypting sensitive credentials at rest (AES-256-GCM)
- Building full-stack SaaS with modern tools (Next.js 16, Prisma 7, Supabase)

---

<div style="margin-top: 2rem; display: flex; gap: 1.5rem; flex-wrap: wrap; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center;">
<a href="../problems.qmd" style="display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem;">
  <i class="fa-solid fa-arrow-left"></i> Back to Problems
</a>
<a href="../about.qmd" class="btn btn-primary">
  About Me <i class="fa-solid fa-arrow-right" style="margin-left: 0.5rem;"></i>
</a>
</div>
